text: Upon entering, I notice the silence and sterility of the glass and metal—a kind of anticipation hums in the air, heightened by the spectacle of light reflecting from the walls, making everything feel transparent and exposed. Moving inside, the vibrant, calculated displays of R+D surround me, immersing me in a world built for thought and creation. I feel curiosity stir within, but also an odd sense of detachment, as if I am both invited to participate and expected merely to observe. Here, innovation hangs thick—somewhere between promise and performance, reality and abstraction.
